t3.ExpDetail: Unable to analyze due to insufficient data

t5.ExpAdvice: No result is returned after timeout, please test again!

t01010101.ExpAdvice: There may be compatibility issues with third-party applications and systems. It is recommended to upgrade to the latest version.

t01010102.ExpAdvice: There may be compatibility issues with third-party applications and systems. It is recommended to upgrade to the latest version.

t01010103.ExpAdvice: If the underlying software service encounters an abnormality, or the CPU/memory is unstable, it may cause multiple abnormal restarts. It is recommended to flash to the latest version before using it. If the phenomenon persists, it is recommended to replace the watch motherboard.

t01010104.ExpAdvice: Occasional underlying restarts may be due to compatibility issues with third-party applications and systems. It is recommended to upgrade or flash to the latest version.

t01010105.ExpAdvice: If the bottom layer restarts and the upper layer software restarts at the same time, it may be a compatibility issue with third-party applications and systems. It is recommended to upgrade to the latest version.

t01010106.ExpAdvice: Frequent bottom-layer restarts and upper-layer software restarts occur at the same time. It may be caused by an abnormality in the underlying software service or CPU/memory instability. It is recommended to flash the machine to the latest version before using it. If the problem persists, it is recommended to send it to an advanced maintenance center.

t01010601.ExpAdvice: Ask the user whether they have installed third-party applications that are not in the software store. If so, suggest the user to uninstall these third-party applications. Otherwise, users are advised to upgrade to the latest version. If the subsequent phenomenon still exists, it may be caused by the user's machine being used for a long time and the application occupying too much memory. It is recommended that the user restart the watch or re-flash to the latest version. (The flash will clear user data, please consult the user’s wishes in advance).

t01010901.ExpAdvice: If the system is stuck at startup, it may be due to abnormal system files or compatibility issues between third-party applications and the system. It is recommended to flash to the latest version.

t01020101.ExpAdvice: Ask the user if they pressed the button or the crown by mistake. Pressing the crown for a long time will vibrate. If the user presses the crown by mistake and a hard restart occurs, the user can be guided to wear the watch strap loosely or move the watch slightly toward the arm. Some; if the user actively long-presses the button or the crown to trigger a hard restart, the user will be asked whether it is caused by system lag, and asked whether the user has installed applications from non-software stores. If so, the user can be advised to uninstall these non-software stores. application. Otherwise, it is recommended to flash to the latest version.

t01020102.ExpAdvice: Ask the user if they pressed the button or the crown by mistake. Pressing the crown for a long time will vibrate. If the user presses the crown by mistake and a hard restart occurs, the user can be guided to wear the watch strap loosely or move the watch slightly toward the arm. Some; if the user actively long-presses the button or the crown to trigger a hard restart, the user will be asked whether it is caused by system lag, and asked whether the user has installed applications from non-software stores. If so, the user can be advised to uninstall these non-software stores. application. Otherwise, it is recommended to flash to the latest version.

t04020101.ExpAdvice: When the watch is being charged, some electrical energy is converted into heat, and it is normal to generate heat.

t04020102.ExpAdvice: Users are reminded not to play music in a charging environment.
t04020102.ExpDetail: Prolonged music playback while the watch is charging.

t04020103.ExpAdvice: Users are reminded not to play music with the screen turned on for long periods of time.
t04020103.ExpDetail: Prolonged music playback with the watch while the screen on.

t04020104.ExpAdvice: Users are reminded not to make calls while charging.
t04020104.ExpDetail: Prolonged call on the watch while charging.

t04020105.ExpAdvice: Users are reminded not to make calls while the screen is on for a long time.
t04020105.ExpDetail: Prolonged call on the watch with the screen on.

t04020106.ExpAdvice: Users are reminded to turn off the screen when charging.
t04020106.ExpDetail: Watch screen kept on for too long while charging.

t04020107.ExpAdvice: Users are reminded not to perform download operations in a charging environment.
t04020107.ExpDetail: Prolonged data download on the watch while charging.

t04020108.ExpAdvice: Users are reminded not to perform downloading operations with the screen turned on for a long time.
t04020108.ExpDetail: Prolonged data download on the watch with the screen on.

t04020109.ExpAdvice: Poor watch network signal will cause power consumption. Please consult the local network operator, or contact the local network operator for on-site testing.

t04020110.ExpAdvice: Upload the log to the background for analysis.

t04020111.ExpAdvice: The application is a high-load application, and it is normal for the machine to heat up due to long-term use.

t04020112.ExpAdvice: Ask the user whether they actively turn on navigation, use third-party software that uses navigation for a long time, or do frequent outdoor sports (running, walking, etc.). If so, it is a normal fever phenomenon. Otherwise, please upload the log to the background for analysis.

t04020113.ExpAdvice: It is normal for the phone to become hot due to prolonged phone calls.

t04020114.ExpAdvice: Calling when the watch network signal is poor will cause power consumption. Please ask the user whether the call quality is stable.

t04020115.ExpAdvice: Please consult the user's usage scenarios, combine power-consuming applications and power-consuming diagnosis and troubleshooting, and use a DC regulated power supply to detect whether the watch hardware is leaking.

t04020116.ExpAdvice: The watch will download data for a long time when performing OTA upgrades, downloading and installing applications, etc., and the resulting heat is normal.
t04020116.ExpDetail: Prolonged data download on the watch.

t05050100.ExpAdvice: The RF transmission path has been completed. To ensure the normal use of watch functions, the diagnostic tool will automatically restart the watch.

t05050103.ExpAdvice: Please stop using the watch eSIM and test again. Method: Mobile Health App - Device - eSIM Management - Settings - Suspend eSIM.

t05050106.ExpAdvice: The test cannot be repeated, please restart and test again.

t05050107.ExpAdvice: Please make sure the watch has sufficient power before trying the test again.

t05050108.ExpAdvice: The watch is fetching logs, please stop fetching logs and retest. Method: Watch dial - enter *#800# - click to stop grabbing.

t05050102.ExpAdvice:
1. Please confirm whether the watch is accidentally touched during the test.
2. Testing again is still abnormal, which means there is a problem with some frequency bands. Please replace the watch motherboard.

t06010001.ExpAdvice: Check whether the system positioning switch is turned off.
t06010001.ExpDetail: There is too little GPS data to diagnose. The positioning may not be possible because the positioning switch is turned off.

t06010101.ExpAdvice:
Try to do it in an open environment (there are no high-rise buildings within 50 meters nearby, please do not do it in the building if you have no conditions). When the watch is not worn, enter *#802# on the dial to check the star search results. If no less than 4 are found, satellites and the average signal of the 4 satellites with the largest signal value is not less than 30. The equipment is normal;
Otherwise *#*#700# upgrade the GPS and then conduct the signal test; if it still fails, please inspect the GPS hardware.
t06010101.ExpDetail: Using GPS in an environment where high-rise buildings are densely packed and blocked causes poor star search signals.

t06010102.ExpAdvice: Detect whether the system positioning switch is turned off; ask the user which application cannot be located, and check the positioning permission of the application.
t06010102.ExpDetail: GPS is not turned on or application permissions are not turned on.

t06010103.ExpAdvice: Check whether the system positioning switch is turned off.
t06010103.ExpDetail: There is too little GPS data to diagnose. The positioning may not be possible because the positioning switch is turned off.

t06010104.ExpAdvice:
Try to use it in an open environment (there are no high-rise buildings within 50 meters nearby, please do not do it in the building if you have no conditions). When the watch is not worn, enter *#802# on the dial to view the star search results. No less than 4 can be found. satellites and the average signal of the four satellites with the largest signal value is not less than 30;
Otherwise *#*#700# After upgrading the GPS, perform the signal test again; the GPS hardware still fails to be repaired.
t06010104.ExpDetail: GPS hardware problems or caused by using GPS indoors.

t06010105.ExpAdvice: Please check whether the GPS antenna is damaged, deformed, or has poor contact, etc.

t06030300.ExpAdvice:
Please provide corresponding diagnostic suggestions based on the proportion of disconnection causes per day in the chart:
1. The mobile phone watch is not compatible with Bluetooth. Diagnosis suggestions: Please upload diagnostic logs to feedback to engineers for follow-up.
2. The mobile phone and watch are too far away/blocked by walls. Diagnosis suggestion: This is a normal phenomenon. Please keep your phone and watch within the Bluetooth connection range.
3. The pairing information of the mobile phone and watch is lost. Diagnosis suggestion: Please ignore the watch device in "Mobile Settings - Bluetooth", click "Reconnect" in "Health App - Device" (above the watch face), and click "Confirm" in the pop-up pairing confirmation box.
4. There are too many Bluetooth devices bound to the mobile phone. Diagnostic advice: Unpair unused devices as needed.
5. The mobile watch connection already exists. Diagnosis suggestion: Please turn off the Bluetooth of your phone and try to connect again.
6. The mobile phone refused to connect. Diagnosis suggestion: Some brands of mobile phones have a Bluetooth blacklist function. Please remove the watch from the Bluetooth blacklist of the mobile phone.
7. The reconnection parameter is not supported. Diagnosis suggestions: Please upload diagnostic logs to feedback to engineers for follow-up.
8. Turn off the mobile phone’s Bluetooth. Diagnosis suggestion: Please keep the Bluetooth of your mobile phone turned on.
9. The watch Bluetooth is turned off. Diagnosis suggestion: Please keep the watch Bluetooth turned on.
10. The mobile health app was cleaned in the background. Diagnosis suggestion: Please follow the tutorial in "Health - Device - More Functions - Anti-disconnection Settings" to keep alive.
11. Bluetooth stability problem. Diagnosis suggestion: Please flash to the latest version. If the problem still exists if you continue to use it, please upload logs and feedback to engineers for follow-up.
12. The problem of small core stability. Diagnosis suggestion: Please flash to the latest version. If the problem still exists if you continue to use it, please upload logs and feedback to engineers for follow-up.

t09010102.ExpAdvice:
If the diagnosis result is not normal, please enter *#899# on the watch dial - Manual test - Equipment debugging - TouchScreen - Touch screen automatic test and re-test. After clicking to enter, the watch will automatically test. Wait for about 10 seconds to display Pass, which means it is normal; If the result is not Pass, please follow the steps below to continue
1. Flash your phone to the latest version.
2. If the watch system is already the latest version, please restart the watch and retest. If the problem still cannot be solved, it is recommended to send it to an advanced maintenance center for testing.

t30010100.ExpAdvice:
1. Wearing the watch too loosely will cause the watch to shake while sleeping or running, causing the wrist to fall off for a very short period of time, causing the screen to lock, which is normal.
2. When the body temperature is low at the beginning of running, the sensor signal quality is poor and the heart rate may be inaccurate, which is normal.
3. Sweating during exercise causes the watch to slip or the watch is worn too loosely, resulting in poor sensor signal quality and possibly inaccurate heart rate, which is normal.
4. Blood oxygen/heart rate measurement is affected by many factors such as the tightness and position of the watch, wrist hair, tattoos, wrist circumference, external temperature, etc. Inaccuracies may occur, which is normal.

t30010101.ExpAdvice:
1. Restart the watch and try again;
2. Flash to the latest version;
3. If the problem still exists, please enter "*#899#" to detect the PPG sensor.
t30010101.ExpDetail: System lag leads to a high rate of PPG sensor loss, which may lead to abnormalities in wear, heart rate, and blood oxygen.

t30020100.ExpAdvice:
1. The watch performs step counting and recognition based on arm swing movements. If the arm swing is too small when walking, the step counting may be inaccurate.
2. Taking various means of transportation may affect the accuracy of step counting.
3. Affected by swimming movements, recognition errors may occur, which is normal.

t30020101.ExpAdvice: 1. Restart the watch and try again; 2. Flash to the latest version; 3. If the problem still exists, please enter "*#899#" to detect the gyroscope.
t30020101.ExpDetail: System lagging causes a high rate of ACC point loss, which may lead to abnormal performance in step counting, heart rate, mileage, swimming, and ball sports.

t30030100.ExpAdvice: Affected by swimming movements, recognition errors may occur, which is normal.

t30030101.ExpAdvice:
1. Restart the watch and try again;
2. Upgrade the watch to the latest version;
3. If the problem still exists, please use the tool model to further measure whether the hardware is normal.
t30030101.ExpDetail: ystem lags cause a high rate of air pressure loss, which may cause abnormal performance in mountain climbing, altitude, swimming, and sports counting.

t30040100.ExpAdvice: Affected by factors such as external magnetic fields, the magnetometer may be inaccurate, resulting in inaccurate trajectory and swimming. This is a normal phenomenon.

t30040101.ExpAdvice:
1. Restart the watch and try again;
2. Upgrade the watch to the latest version;
3. If the problem still exists, please enter "*#899#" to detect geomagnetism.
t30040101.ExpDetail: ystem lag causes the magnetometer to lose points at a high rate, which may lead to abnormal pace and trajectory performance.

t30050100.ExpAdvice: Due to factors such as high-rise buildings, glass curtain walls, trees, and dark clouds, GPS positioning may be inaccurate, resulting in inaccurate trajectory and mileage. This is a normal phenomenon.

t30050101.ExpAdvice:
1. Restart the watch and try again;
2. Upgrade the watch to the latest version;
3. If the problem still exists, please check GPS for diagnosis.
t30050101.ExpDetail: System lag causes a high GPS point loss rate, which may lead to abnormal pace, mileage, and trajectory performance.

t06040501.ExpAdvice:
If users have feedback issues, please follow the steps below to continue troubleshooting.
1. Confirm whether the NFC switch is turned on (Settings - Connection & Sharing - NFC).
2. Check whether the current card type in the user's watch wallet is consistent with the target card swiping type. For example, the first card currently opened by the watch wallet is an access control card, but it needs to be swiped at the bus or subway gate.
3. If the user reports that the bus card swiping fails, it is recommended that the user retry the card swiping or change the gate to swipe the card. If the card still cannot be swiped, please confirm with the subway manual customer service.
4. If the user reports that the access control card fails to be swiped, please confirm whether the access control is blocked. You can try swiping the card at different angles and distances.

t06040502.ExpAdvice:
1. Confirm whether a useful card (bus card, access card, or the camera position of an OPPO mobile phone with a card) is placed against the watch during the diagnosis process
2. Restart the watch and test again.
3. It is recommended to flash to the latest version and retest.
4. If the problem still cannot be solved, it is recommended to send it to an advanced maintenance center for testing.

t06040101.ExpAdvice:
1. Guide the user to record the card according to the optimal card reading position as prompted by the card recording tutorial of the Health App.
2. Please confirm whether the user's card works in the NFC band (try to use the OPPO mobile wallet app to record the card to verify).
3. Restart the watch and try recording again.

t06040102.ExpAdvice:
1. The detection times out and confirm whether the card is close to the watch (please keep the card close to the watch according to the optimal card reading position indicated in the card recording tutorial of the Health App).
2. Restart the watch and test again.
3. It is recommended to flash the watch to the latest version, upgrade the mobile health app to the latest version and then test again.
4. If the problem still cannot be solved, the watch NFC antenna needs to be replaced.

t08099901.ExpAdvice:
How to deal with speaker noise:
1. Check the watch audio hardware
Please connect the after-sales diagnostic tool to check whether the speakers, microphones and Bluetooth audio devices are normal, and perform diagnostic and repair according to the after-sales tool diagnostic recommendations.
2. Probabilistic noise from the watch
If the audio hardware of the watch detects normally, but probabilistic noise occurs, you can troubleshoot as follows:
2.1 Detection of clogging of watch balance film
- Detection method:
Place the watch on the heating table at 40-50 degrees Celsius and heat it for 5 minutes. If there is no heating table, play music continuously for 30 minutes, and then use the watch to play music again. If the speaker is abnormal at this time, it is judged that the speaker balance film is blocked.
- Repair method for speaker balance diaphragm plugging:
(1) Clean the watch body according to the SOP for square meters, and then check the effect; if the problem of blocked holes and noise cannot be solved, replace the middle frame;
(2) Please replace the back cover of the round watch.
2.2 Abnormal watch software or signal
Software or signal noise problems are complicated and the reasons cannot be given directly. Please capture the watch audio log and submit the problem feedback to the engineers for processing.

t06040701.ExpAdvice:
1. If there is a return value and FAIL, please check whether the watch is placed on a magnetic base or there are magnetic substances around it. If not, you need to disassemble the watch and check the antenna connection.
2. No return value and FAIL, please restart the watch and test.

t10900101.ExpAdvice:
1. Remove the back shell for cleaning, reinstall the back shell and retest.
2. If there is still a problem in the first step of testing, please replace the back shell. And install the problematic back case to other normal watches to cross-check to confirm whether the back case is faulty.
3. Send the problematic rear shell back to the headquarters for analysis.

t10910101.ExpAdvice:
1. If only the ECG sensor lead test is abnormal, please cross-test whether the bottom case electrodes, side electrodes and bottom case shrapnel are in normal contact with the leaking copper in the middle frame, and determine to replace the faulty hardware.
2. If only the ECG path test is abnormal, the bottom case needs to be replaced.
3. If only the ECG short-circuit test is abnormal, you can first wipe the electric shock and electric shock gap on the bottom case with an alcohol cloth, dry it and then test again. If the problem is still abnormal, the bottom case needs to be replaced.
4. If multiple problems are detected above, please cross-verify to determine the number of problems and proceed accordingly.

t06010501.ExpAdvice:
1. If the GPS firmware is normal, please replace the hardware.
2. If the GPS firmware is abnormal, flash to the latest version.

t06011200.ExpDetail: GPS star search function is normal.

t06011201.ExpAdvice:
1. Perform the following tests: first connect to the Android phone and wait 30 seconds to ensure that the phone can connect to the Internet.
Enter *#802#, take the watch to an open area for positioning, and observe the number of star searches and CN0.
(1) If it continues to be less than 4, or CN0 is less than 20db, refresh the latest watch version, connect to the mobile phone and test again
(2) If the positioning can be successful, and the number of searched stars is greater than 8, and CN0 is greater than 20db, the GPS function is normal.
Please explain to users: When the watch is connected to a mobile phone, it can obtain auxiliary positioning information from the mobile phone to achieve faster positioning speed and more accurate positioning trajectory.
t06011201.ExpDetail: The number of star searches is too small, which may be due to abnormal auxiliary data, weak GPS environment, or damaged hardware.

t06011301.ExpAdvice:
1. First connect the Android phone and wait 30 seconds to ensure that the phone can connect to the Internet. Enter *#802#, take the watch to an open area for positioning, and observe the number of star searches and CN0.
A. If it continues to be less than 4, or CN0 is less than 20db, re-flash the version, connect to the mobile phone and test again. If it still exists, replace the hardware.
B. If the positioning can be successful, the number of searched stars is greater than 8, and the CN0 is greater than 20db, then the GPS function is normal.
Please explain to users: When the watch is connected to a mobile phone, it can obtain auxiliary positioning information from the mobile phone to achieve faster positioning speed and more accurate positioning trajectory.
t06011301.ExpDetail: Maybe the GPS environment is poor or the hardware is damaged.

t06011302.ExpAdvice: Users have insufficient GPS data and are advised to use more GPS applications.

t06011303.ExpAdvice: Users have insufficient GPS data and are advised to use more GPS applications.

t06011400.ExpAdvice: The user has not turned off the location service switch.

t06011401.ExpAdvice: Please turn on location services in the watch "Settings - Permissions and Privacy".

t06011402.ExpAdvice: If the application GPS fails to open, please turn on the location service in the watch "Settings - Permissions and Privacy".

t06011403.ExpAdvice: If the application GPS fails to open, please turn on the location service in the watch "Settings - Permissions and Privacy".

t01060101.ExpAdvice: Installing unofficial third-party applications may cause system lags, unsmooth operations, brief black screens, and unresponsive operations. It can return to normal after flashing or restoring factory settings.
t01060101.ExpDetail: Unofficial third-party applications cause system lag or instability.

t11010102.ExpAdvice:
If the diagnosis result is not normal, please enter *#899# on the watch dial - After-sales Tools - Keypad Test and re-test. Follow the steps to click the button, click the crown, and turn the crown.; otherwise, follow the steps below Continue the operation
1. Flash to the latest version
2. If the watch system is already the latest version, please restart the watch and retest. If the problem still cannot be solved, it is recommended to send it to an advanced maintenance center for testing.
t11010102.ExpDetail: Clicking the crown does not respond.
